```asp
% Define the constants
game("Angry Ants"; "Bubble Bombs"; "Flowcarts"; "Peter Parka"; "Wizard World").
month(3; 4; 5; 6; 7).
developer("Apptastic"; "Digibits"; "Gadingo"; "Novio"; "Vortia").

% Each game is released in exactly one month and by exactly one developer
{released(G, M, D)} :- game(G), month(M), developer(D).

% Each month has exactly one game released
:- #count{G, D : released(G, M, D)} != 1, month(M).

% Each developer has exactly one game
:- #count{G, M : released(G, M, D)} != 1, developer(D).

% Each game is released by exactly one developer
:- #count{M, D : released(G, M, D)} != 1, game(G).

% Clue 1: The app released by Vortia was released 2 months later than Flowcarts
:- released(G1, M1, "Vortia"), released(G2, M2, "Flowcarts"), M1 != M2 + 2.

% Clue 2: Angry Ants was released 3 months later than the app released by Gadingo
:- released("Angry Ants", M1, D1), released(G2, M2, "Gadingo"), M1 != M2 + 3.

% Clue 3: The game released by Apptastic was released 3 months later than Flowcarts
:- released(G1, M1, "Apptastic"), released(G2, M2, "Flowcarts"), M1 != M2 + 3.

% Clue 4: The game released in July was made by Apptastic
:- released(G, 7, D), D != "Apptastic".

% Clue 5: Peter Parka is either the app released by Novio or the game released in May
:- released("Peter Parka", M, D), M != 5, D != "Novio".

% Ensure all games, months, and developers are used exactly once
% This is enforced by the cardinality constraints above
```

```asp
employee("Herbert"; "Marc"; "Nathan"; "Victor").
rider(50; 75; 100; 125).
section("green"; "pink"; "purple"; "red").

% Each employee is assigned exactly one rider and one section
1 {match(E, R, S) : rider(R), section(S)} 1 :- employee(E).

% No duplicate assignments in any category
:- match(E1, R1, S1), match(E2, R2, S2), E1 != E2, R1 = R2.
:- match(E1, R1, S1), match(E2, R2, S2), E1 != E2, S1 = S2.
:- match(E1, R1, S1), match(E2, R2, S2), R1 != R2, S1 = S2.

% 1. Herbert served 25 fewer riders than Marc

% 2. The worker who served 125 riders works in the red section

% 3.1 The person who works in the pink section and the person who served 100 riders are different
:- match(E, R, S), R = 100, S = "pink".

% 3.2 The person who works in the pink section is Herbert or Marc
:- match(E, _, "pink"), E != "Herbert", E != "Marc".

% 3.3 The person who served 100 riders is Herbert or Marc
:- match(E, R, _), R = 100, E != "Herbert", E != "Marc".
```

```asp
% This means Victor cannot be in both categories simultaneously
```
